## README excerpt (first ~3 KB)

> # 👤 FaceNet Inference API
>
> A streamlined implementation of FaceNet for face recognition, featuring a pre-trained PyTorch backend and a FastAPI server.
>
> ---
>
> ## 🛠 Setup Instructions
>
> ### 1. Download Model Weights
> Before running the application, you must download the pre-trained weights.
> * **Model:** [VGGFace2 (Inception ResNet V1)](https://github.com/timesler/facenet-pytorch/releases/download/v2.2.9/20180402-114759-vggface2.pt)
> * **Destination:** Save the `.pt` file inside the `model_resources/` directory.
>
> #### 📂 Project Structure
> ```
> ├── model_resources/          # Pre-trained model weights (.pt)
> ├── prediction_models/
> │   ├── api_server.py         # FastAPI implementation
> │   └── test/
>         └── facecrop.txt      # 1 facecropped base64 encoded image
> │       └── test_api.py       # Integration tests
> ├── req.txt                   # Dependency list
> └── README.md                 # Project documentation
> ```
>
> ### 2. Environment Configuration
> This project requires **Python 3.10**. It is recommended to use a virtual environment.
>
> ```bash
> # Create and activate environment
> python3.10 -m venv venv
> source venv/bin/activate  
>
> # Install dependencies
> pip install -r req.txt
> ```
>
> ### 3. Start the API Server
> The server uses Gunicorn with Uvicorn workers for robust, asynchronous request handling.
> From the prediction_modules directory, run:
>
> ```bash
> gunicorn -w 1 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker api_server:app --bind 0.0.0.0:8001
> ```
>
> ```
> 🚀 API Endpoints Summary
>
> Method	Endpoint	Description
> POST	/predict	Accepts an image and returns face embeddings.
> GET	/health	        Returns the current status of the server.
> ```
>
> ### 4. Verification
> To ensure the API is responding correctly, execute the automated test script:
> ```bash
> python prediction_modules/test/test_api.py
> ```
